soma.insight_of_week: Founders Pledge USD 5M decision window opens in 6 weeks (decision expected Q1-Q2 2027, but due diligence response cycle closes August 15). Parallel: Vadim (FP member / impact investor) is now aware of LearnImpact but must be warmed through Lorcan only—do not direct outreach.

hot_list.why_now: DD response window closes August 15. Youdi's KiuFunza research quality answers (Q2.1-3.2) are the throttle on probability. Early submission signals confidence and keeps FP evaluation on track for Q1-Q2 2027 decision.
hot_list.recommended_action: Youdi to finalize KiuFunza research responses by August 10. Michael to schedule light check-in call with Lorcan by August 5 (not a push, just 'wanted to confirm we're on track for your timeline'). Do not mention Vadim.

hot_list.why_now: Ali Ellegard is warm post-Track 1 win ($100K). Track 2 conversation has NOT started. VTF SOMA momentum (post-signing) creates perfect timing to frame SOMA AI coaching as 'assessment → coaching loop' narrative. 10-year partnership potential.
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael to email Ali by July 21 with 2-pager: 'Track 1 gave us assessment baseline; SOMA WhatsApp AI coaching (Mwalimu) closes the improvement cycle. Natural partnership progression. Can we grab 30 min in September?' Frame as complementary, not competing.

hot_list.why_now: KiuFunza Grant Agreement signing imminent (target: July 10). ONLY AFTER signing should SOMA conversation begin. VTF 80% tech mandate (WhatsApp, mobile, AI) maps perfectly to Mwalimu. Sandra's concern re: field allocation balance is resolved; relationship is 'hot'. Timing is critical: do not conflate the two asks.
hot_list.recommended_action: After VTF signing confirmed (expect July 10-15), Michael to schedule separate 1:1 with Sandra by July 25: 'KiuFunza will strengthen teacher baseline; SOMA AI coaching accelerates their improvement. Can we explore a complementary SOMA conversation in August?' Position as additive, not cannibalistic.

hot_list.why_now: Google for Nonprofits ad grant ($120K/year, rolling application) has immediate implementation path: LearnImpact registration (confirmed: 00NGO/R/8931) + domain (learnimpact.org secured) + website live = Goodstack submission in 10-14 days. This unlocks credibility for subsequent Google.org Impact Challenge cultivation.
hot_list.recommended_action: Confirm website launch date (target: July 31). Submit Google for Nonprofits application to Goodstack immediately upon live site (estimated Aug 1-5). Activate $10K/month search credits + free Workspace for comms/fundraising team. Parallel: Begin tracking Google.org Impact Challenge Africa calendar for next open call (typically announced 3-6 months in advance). This relationship-building is prerequisite for $500K-$2M Impact Challenge ask.

hot_list.why_now: KiuFunza renewal signal spontaneous (May 2026). Hearing Committee is live. Grant Agreement momentum is strong. Probability 70%. Effort is minimal (relationship is partner-stage). No risk—only upside is solidifying continuity before budgeting cycles lock in 2027.
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael to send brief thank-you note to Hempel relationship manager by July 20: 'Steering Committee momentum is strong; Hearing Committee process is underway. We are on track for Phase 5 and will update you on any milestone shifts.' Keep it light. Relationship management only.

hot_list.why_now: Tanzania GPE grant ($88M) approved 2026. SOMA can be positioned as complementary NGO delivery mechanism for foundational learning. Requires PMO-RALG door-opener (government touchpoint). This is a 6-month development cycle, not urgent, but requires relationship warm-up by September to influence year-end government coordination meetings.
hot_list.recommended_action: By July 31, Michael to identify and brief one PMO-RALG contact (ask Kibaha District Council if they have a relationship) who could introduce SOMA as 'NGO complement to GPE-funded foundational learning.' Target: initial conversation with GPE Tanzania coordinator by September 15. Frame: 'How can LearnImpact's evidence-based AI coaching extend GPE's reach in Mtwara RCT sites?'

hot_list.why_now: Schmidt Futures is increasing focus on AI in education (2025-2026 signal). SOMA is a perfect fit: novel AI application (WhatsApp chatbot), underserved communities (rural Tanzania), scientific rigor (Kibaha pilot → Mtwara RCT). Alignment 87%, but probability 32% (long sales cycle). Effort is moderate. Cultivating now plants flag for late 2026 / early 2027 ask.
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael to commission 1-pager on Mwalimu AI architecture, cost-effectiveness vs. Gates AI-education partnerships, and Kibaha pilot early results (expected July-August). By August 15, pitch researcher relationship at Schmidt Futures (not major gift officer) with 'We've built a micro-cost AI coaching tool for rural teachers; worth a conversation?' Goal: exploratory call by September. Low-pressure cultivation.

new_funders_detected.name: Vadim (via Founders Pledge / Impact Investor Network)
new_funders_detected.why_it_fits: High-net-worth FP member with personal giving capacity. Introduced via Lorcan Clarke. If FP recommends LearnImpact, Vadim may direct separate personal investment. Does not require independent cultivation—flows through Lorcan relationship.
new_funders_detected.action: Do not initiate direct outreach. Monitor through Lorcan updates. If Lorcan signals Vadim interest, let Lorcan orchestrate introduction. Potential major gift ($500K+) contingent on FP recommendation.

priority_shifts.what_changed: Relationship moved from 'KiuFunza partner' to 'hot SOMA prospect' post-documentary visit (June 29). Sandra's public commitment signal (field visits, communications) raises VTF's organizational confidence and appetite for follow-on programmes. Tech mandate alignment is now visible internal priority.
priority_shifts.action: Do not initiate SOMA conversation until KiuFunza signing is complete. After July 15, position SOMA AI coaching as separate growth initiative, not budget reallocation. VTF's 80% tech mandate is explicit match for WhatsApp chatbot. Probability should increase from 80% to 85%+ once SOMA framing lands.

priority_shifts.what_changed: Prerequisite completion (NGO registration + domain) has unlocked immediate Grant Programme pathway (Google for Nonprofits rolling application). This was a gate; it is now removed. Simultaneously, Google.org Impact Challenge Africa remains dormant (no open call) but SOMA's AI hook aligns with 2025-2026 Google AI for social good priority shift.
priority_shifts.action: Immediate: execute Google for Nonprofits application (deadline operationally: August 5). Medium-term: establish relationship with Google.org Africa team through ad grant success; build credibility for Impact Challenge cultivation when call opens (watch for announcement Aug 2026-Jan 2027).

soma.positioning_advice: Over the next 30 days (July 14 – August 14), Michael should position LearnImpact as the 'evidence-meets-tech' organization in three channels: (1) Publish one 600-word blog post on Mwalimu WhatsApp AI coaching architecture + Kibaha early results (target: dev.to, Medium, or LearnImpact blog by July 31). Frame: 'How we built a $3.12/student AI teacher coach in rural Tanzania.' This signals technical credibility to Schmidt Futures and Google.org scouts. (2) Record a 90-second video testimonial from one Kibaha teacher or district coordinator (Kibaha District Council) on Mwalimu's classroom impact (target: live on website by Aug 15). This humanizes SOMA for Founders Pledge, Dovetail, and GPE evaluators. (3) In August, Michael should speak at one education tech or Africa-focused event (webinar, panel, or convening) on 'AI at scale in low-income education: lessons from Tanzania.' This keeps LearnImpact visible to prospect tier-1 funders (Gates, ADQ, Open Philanthropy) without hard asks. Goal: by late August, when Founders Pledge decision cycle accelerates and Dovetail Track 2 conversation opens, Michael is positioned as the voice on evidence-backed AI education in East Africa—not just a proposal writer.

kiufunza.insight_of_week: VTF KiuFunza Phase 5 signing is imminent (deadline 2026-07-15, one day away). Sandra Oswald confirmed readiness to sign TZS 462M (~USD 178K) as of 2026-07-01. This is a critical moment: (1) Confirm signing completion TODAY or tomorrow to lock in USD 178K; (2) DO NOT mention SOMA to VTF until after signing is complete — Sandra's documentary team visit to Pangani & Kilindi signals strong public commitment, which is leverage for a separate SOMA conversation post-signing; (3) Prepare VTF onboarding pack immediately to show momentum and relationship health.

hot_list.why_now: Signing imminent. USD 178K KiuFunza Phase 5 confirmed. Agreement finalised 2026-07-01. Deadline is 2026-07-15 (one day). This is a MUST-WIN, signing-stage opportunity.
hot_list.recommended_action: Contact Sandra Oswald today to confirm signing date and location. Prepare partner welcome pack with: (a) KiuFunza Phase 5 implementation roadmap; (b) VTF comms team field visit schedule; (c) preliminary SOMA tech alignment brief (hold for post-signing conversation). Lock in signing before EOW.

hot_list.why_now: Anchor donor renewal under active Hearing Committee review. USD 587K FY2026 confirmed. Rosa's unprompted renewal signal at May 2026 Steering Committee indicates strong relationship. Hearing Committee deadline is 2026-08-31 (48 days). No further push needed — relationship is warm and protected.
hot_list.recommended_action: Provide Hearing Committee with ANY additional materials requested by Rosa by 2026-07-31. Do not over-communicate. Protect relationship health above all other donor interactions. Brief Michael on Hearing Committee members' likely priorities (cost-effectiveness, learning outcomes, Tanzania government partnership).

hot_list.why_now: Track 1 USD 100K confirmed. Track 2 (SOMA) conversation NOT yet started. Ali Ellegard is warm post-Track 1 and ready for next ask. Potential 10-year partnership signal. Recommended action window is 2026-09-01 (49 days).
hot_list.recommended_action: Schedule call with Ali Ellegard by 2026-08-15. Frame Track 2 SOMA pitch as: 'Track 1 assessment gave us the baseline; Track 2 AI coaching closes the feedback loop.' Emphasize Mwalimu WhatsApp chatbot as tech-enabled teacher support (aligns with Dovetail's assessment technology priority). Prepare USD 100K–150K ask for SOMA Year 1 pilot in 10 Kibaha schools.

hot_list.why_now: USD 5M SOMA proposal under full review. DD questions submitted by Lorcan Clarke (deadline passed 2026-06-26). Michael & Youdi have responded. Lorcan's feedback on SOMA CN was positive ('strong concept note'). Decision expected Q1–Q2 2027 but momentum is warm NOW. Effort to maintain is low (let Lorcan lead).
hot_list.recommended_action: Do NOT over-communicate with Lorcan. Follow up only if no word by 2026-08-15. Let Lorcan set the pace. Keep Youdi brief on any new DD requirements (KiuFunza research lane only — maintain clean separation). Prepare for potential Lorcan + Vadim parallel track: allow Lorcan to orchestrate Vadim introduction; do NOT reach out to Vadim directly.

hot_list.why_now: Tier 1 prospect with very high alignment (85) on RCT evidence, government integration, cost-effectiveness, scale pathway. Probability is moderate (38%) but effort is justified. No recent news — cold outreach required. Arnold Ventures has moved significantly into education evidence-based policy post-2024.
hot_list.recommended_action: Research Arnold Ventures' 2026 education portfolio. Identify education programme officer. Prepare targeted intro via warm introduction (ask Founders Pledge or Hempel if they have Arnold Ventures connection). Lead with Mtwara RCT design (70 schools, 2027), QJE publication on KiuFunza (+9.4pp), and USD 3.12/student/year LAYS metric. Position as evidence-backed scale opportunity.

hot_list.why_now: Global Coalition for Foundational Learning announced 2026 with $15B commitment through 2030. World Bank alignment score is 76 on LAYS, foundational learning, government systems. Probability is moderate (30%) but effort is high (5). This is a strategic opportunity for 2027–2030 scale pathway.
hot_list.recommended_action: Request brief call with World Bank Tanzania education specialist to discuss LearnImpact's role in Global Coalition. Lead with: (1) Mtwara RCT 70 schools (2027) — government co-financing model; (2) USD 3.12/student/year LAYS metric; (3) Kibaha Term 3 2026 pilot. Ask about WB's coordination with GoT Ministry of Education on foundational learning priorities. Timeline: initial conversation by 2026-09-30.

hot_list.why_now: Tier 2 prospect with solid alignment (76) on foundational literacy, teacher training, East Africa. Probability is moderate (38%). No recent news — cold outreach. Dubai Cares has historically been active in Tanzania education. Effort is low (3).
hot_list.recommended_action: Research Dubai Cares' 2026–2027 Tanzania strategy. Identify education programme officer. Prepare soft intro highlighting teacher training + foundational learning + East Africa fit. Position SOMA (AI-enabled coaching) as innovative teacher support model. Ask about alignment with Dubai Cares' current grant cycles. Timeline: initial outreach by 2026-08-31.

new_funders_detected.name: Global Coalition for Foundational Learning (World Bank leadership)
new_funders_detected.why_it_fits: USD 15B commitment 2026–2030 focused on foundational learning in low-income countries. LearnImpact's SOMA (foundational literacy via WhatsApp AI) and KiuFunza (teacher incentives for learning outcomes) are direct fit. Tanzania is likely priority country.
new_funders_detected.action: Map World Bank Tanzania education officer and Global Coalition secretariat. Request Letter of Interest template for 2027 call. Position LearnImpact as evidence-backed implementation partner for Tanzania foundational learning workstream.

priority_shifts.what_changed: 2025 US ODA cuts (56.9%) have severely reduced USAID global education funding. Tanzania education programme is now uncertain. Probability score should be downgraded from 18% (already low) to <10%. Effort remains high (5).
priority_shifts.action: DEPRIORITIZE USAID outreach. Flag for quarterly review only. Monitor USAID Tanzania Mission announcements for any foundational literacy RFPs, but do not initiate outreach. Opportunity cost is too high given Hempel, VTF, and Founders Pledge momentum.

kiufunza.positioning_advice: Michael should position LearnImpact as the bridge between evidence-based research (QJE publication, Mtwara RCT pipeline) and government-ready scale (Kibaha Term 3 2026 pilot, Kibaha District Council co-financing, VTF technology mandate alignment). In the next 30 days, Michael should: (1) Publish or speak (webinar/blog) on 'Teacher Coaching at Scale: Why WhatsApp AI Works in Low-Resource Settings' — this positions SOMA as the logical next step after KiuFunza's incentives work. (2) Highlight VTF documentary partnership (Pangani & Kilindi visits) as proof of government + tech funder alignment — share learnings from filming with Arnold Ventures and World Bank contacts. (3) Brief on independence from Twaweza: 'LearnImpact is now a standalone evidence-driven systems org — not a startup, not a consulting firm. We prove, then we partner with government.' This reframe neutralizes any residual GoT sensitivity. (4) Do NOT lead with Twaweza history in any outbound. Ever.

timamu.insight_of_week: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund (TZS 2B) activates THIS MONTH (July 2026). Timamu has 78 days to secure legal registration in Tanzania AND submit Expression of Interest to Ministry of ICT by 2026-09-30. This is the largest domestic funding opportunity available—but only if incorporation is completed by mid-August. Registration delay = missed window.

hot_list.why_now: TZS 2B fund launches THIS MONTH. 78-day deadline to EOI. Requires Tanzania legal registration (prerequisite). Alignment 82%, but deadline urgency is critical. Position as disability-inclusive delivery partner for government infrastructure.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Confirm Tanzania company registration status TODAY with Michael. 2) If not registered: engage corporate lawyer THIS WEEK for expedited incorporation (target: 21 days). 3) Draft government EOI positioning Timamu as disability-inclusive programme implementation partner. 4) Contact Ministry of ICT (ICT focal point) to flag intent and understand selection criteria.

hot_list.why_now: Smallest ask ($50K) with highest accessibility and fastest turnaround (49 days). Rolling deadline, concept-note stage. UK Community Fund just awarded £432K to 12 disability orgs—signal of expanded disability focus. First credible anchor grant for Timamu.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Develop 3-page concept note THIS WEEK: focus on Timamu digital creativity tools for PWDs in Tanzania. 2) Highlight assistive technology + creator economy + Phase 1 outcomes. 3) Submit by 2026-08-15 (15-day buffer before 2026-09-01 deadline). 4) Position as catalyst for larger Microsoft/Ford grants.

hot_list.why_now: Highest alignment (93%), rolling deadline, lowest effort (2). Active $150K programme. Most accessible of Tier 1 funders. Apply immediately after Tanzania registration confirmed.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Confirm Tanzania registration completion date (prerequisite for most funders). 2) Develop LOI: Microsoft AI tools for Timamu assistive technology Phase 1 (3-4 weeks). 3) Submit by 2026-08-31 (buffer before 2026-10-01). 4) Reference Adobe grant (if won) as proof of concept.

hot_list.why_now: April 2025 $15M disability commitment is FIRST in Ford history. 25% disability inclusion mandate across ALL grantmaking. Highest strategic alignment for Phase 2 (gig economy, AI tools). Multi-year potential.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Build 12-month relationship plan (not immediate submission). 2) Michael: publish 1-2 op-eds on disability + creator economy by end Q3 (target Ford's networks). 3) Attend/speak at disability-focused convenings (Ford is listening). 4) Develop full proposal for Q1 2027 ($500K–$2M ask for Phase 2 gig economy + AI).

hot_list.why_now: Second-highest alignment (88%), expanding AI for accessibility commitments 2025–2026. Excellent fit for Phase 1 assistive technology. Mid-effort (3). No deadline detected = rolling.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Research Google.org disability grantmaking contacts (likely tech-focused). 2) Develop 2-page inquiry on Timamu AI accessibility tools for PWDs in East Africa. 3) Target submission: 2026-09-15. 4) Reference Microsoft + Adobe grants as momentum markers.

hot_list.why_now: Tier 1 alignment (87%), East Africa focus, disability-focused (proven track record). No recent news = underexplored. Probability 48% suggests room to move needle. Rolling/no deadline = low urgency.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Scan CBM's recent Tanzania grantee landscape (inform positioning). 2) Develop brief inquiry (2-page) on Timamu assistive technology for PWDs. 3) Target submission: 2026-10-15 (after Adobe/Microsoft). 4) Frame as complementary to CBM's inclusive development work.

hot_list.why_now: Tier 1, disability-focused (proven), economic inclusion mandate, East Africa presence. No recent news = low radar. Probability 45% = moderate lift required.
hot_list.recommended_action: 1) Identify H&I Tanzania country manager (relationship entry point). 2) Schedule exploratory call: Timamu assistive technology + economic inclusion for PWDs. 3) Develop soft inquiry by 2026-09-15. 4) Potential multi-year partnership (not single grant).

new_rfps.title: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund — Disability-Inclusive Programme Partnership
new_rfps.why_it_fits: Timamu's domestic anchor opportunity. TZS 2B Vision 2050 fund launches July 2026. Requires Tanzania legal registration. Position Timamu as disability-inclusive programme delivery partner. Phase 1 digital creation aligns directly with creator economy fund goals.

priority_shifts.what_changed: April 2025: Announced first-ever $15M disability rights commitment + mandate that 25% of ALL Ford grantmaking integrate disability inclusion. This is a structural pivot in Ford's strategy.
priority_shifts.action: Timamu must position as disability + economic inclusion specialist for Phase 2 (gig economy, AI). Michael should develop thought leadership on disability-centered creator economy by Q3 2026. Target multi-year partnership (not single grant).

priority_shifts.what_changed: July 2026: TZS 2B creator economy fund ACTIVATES. First-time domestic large-scale funding for creator economy. Disability inclusion NOT explicitly mentioned in initial framework—major opportunity to embed Timamu as inclusive delivery partner.
priority_shifts.action: Michael + legal team: Secure Tanzania registration by 2026-08-15. Draft EOI emphasizing disability-inclusive digital creation pathways (differentiation from standard creator economy grantees). Contact Ministry of ICT focal point by 2026-07-21.

out_of_industry.why_it_fits: Young Africa Works programme (Mastercard's flagship) is expanding digital livelihoods focus 2025–2026. Creator economy + digital skills are core. Disability inclusion marked as 'emerging' priority. Phase 1 (digital creation) aligns with YAW framework.
out_of_industry.action: Research Mastercard's disability inclusion strategy (likely developing). Develop 1-page inquiry on Timamu PWD creator cohorts by 2026-10-31. Probability 22% = lower-lift prospect, but highest-value if relationship moves.

timamu.positioning_advice: Michael should author or co-publish ONE thought leadership piece by 2026-08-31 on: 'Why Disability-Inclusive Creator Economy Matters for Tanzania Vision 2050.' Target: Op-ed in regional tech/development media + Ford Foundation networks. Key messages: (1) 9–10M Tanzanians with disabilities represent untapped creator talent; (2) Assistive technology + digital tools lower barriers to entry; (3) Gig economy platforms can scale economic inclusion if designed for access. This positions Timamu as strategic partner for Tanzania government (EOI pitch), Ford's disability commitment, and Mastercard's YAW expansion. Simultaneously: schedule 3 informational calls with H&I, CBM, and Google.org by 2026-09-15 (relationship warming ahead of formal submissions). Do NOT wait for registration to complete—begin relationship-building immediately.

cross_programme_conflicts.note: Same funder appears in both LearnImpact and Timamu pools. Coordinate before approaching — never allow simultaneous asks to same contact.
cross_programme_conflicts.recommended_sequence: Decide priority entity first. LearnImpact asks first if active programme ongoing.
